text: First, user needs define individual trees composing stand crown dimensions. data frame must created specific format variables. Light variables plot can defined independently trees cells setting what_trees what_cells arguments. example, setting what_trees = "intercepted" what_cells = "absolute allows better catch differences light energies trees cells. However, careful colours cells, white areas mean cells high-light brighter cells relatively others, confusing without precise legend.

code: exp_design <- expand.grid(   city = c("Madrid", "Brussels", "Oslo"), # Define three towns with different latitudes in Europe   slope = c(0, 20),   aspect = c(0, 180) ) %>%       # Set the longitude/latitude of our three towns   dplyr::mutate(     latitude = case_match(city,                           "Madrid" ~ 40.4167,                           "Brussels" ~ 50.8477,                           "Oslo" ~ 59.9122),     longitude = case_match(city,                           "Madrid" ~ -3.7033,                           "Brussels" ~ 4.3572,                           "Oslo" ~ 10.7313)   ) %>%    dplyr::filter(!(slope == 0 & aspect != 0)) %>% # Because aspect do not change anything in a flat plane   dplyr::mutate(stand_geom = case_when(                   slope == 0 ~ "flat plane",                   slope > 0 & aspect == 0 ~ "north-facing slope",                   slope > 0 & aspect == 180 ~ "south-facing slope"),                 id_simu = as.character(row_number())) %>%    dplyr::relocate(id_simu) #> Warning: There was 1 warning in `dplyr::mutate()`. #> ℹ In argument: `latitude = case_match(...)`. #> Caused by warning: #> ! `case_match()` was deprecated in dplyr 1.2.0. #> ℹ Please use `recode_values()` instead.  exp_design #>   id_simu     city slope aspect latitude longitude         stand_geom #> 1       1   Madrid     0      0  40.4167   -3.7033         flat plane #> 2       2 Brussels     0      0  50.8477    4.3572         flat plane #> 3       3     Oslo     0      0  59.9122   10.7313         flat plane #> 4       4   Madrid    20      0  40.4167   -3.7033 north-facing slope #> 5       5 Brussels    20      0  50.8477    4.3572 north-facing slope #> 6       6     Oslo    20      0  59.9122   10.7313 north-facing slope #> 7       7   Madrid    20    180  40.4167   -3.7033 south-facing slope #> 8       8 Brussels    20    180  50.8477    4.3572 south-facing slope #> 9       9     Oslo    20    180  59.9122   10.7313 south-facing slope

text: unique city (Madrid, Brussels Oslo), create monthly radiation tables containing 12 months information global energy quantity (Hrad) ratio energy diffuse direct energies (DGratio), MJ/m2. important note monthly values correspond radiations received horizontal plane subsequently corrected model estimate incident radiation sloped surfaces.  Across cities, monthly global radiation peaks spring summer due longer daylight higher solar elevation angles, reduce atmospheric scattering favor direct radiation. contrast, winter months combine shorter days, lower sun angles, increased atmospheric path length, leading lower global energy higher diffuse fraction. Madrid exhibits higher global radiation lower diffuse--global ratio due lower latitude, results higher solar elevations, shorter atmospheric path lengths, therefore dominance direct radiation throughout year. However, effect latitude tends plateau: Brussels (50°N) Oslo (60°N) exhibit similar monthly radiation totals diffuse/direct ratios. results compensation lower solar elevation longer day length: high latitudes, summer radiation characterized long-lasting low-angle direct sunlight, can dominate diffuse radiation integrated month. Although total amount partitioning radiation comparable Brussels Oslo, geometric distribution direct rays differs markedly explore later tutorial, strong implications 3D light interception shading patterns.
code: # Get each unique city coordinate coords <- exp_design %>%    dplyr::distinct(city, longitude, latitude)  # Create the list of radiation tables data_rad_list <- vector("list", length = nrow(coords)) names(data_rad_list) <- coords$city  # Fetch  for (i in seq_along(coords)) {      data_rad_list[[coords$city[i]]] <- SamsaRaLight::get_monthly_radiations(     latitude = coords$latitude[i],      longitude = coords$longitude[i]   )      check_monthly_radiations(data_rad_list[[coords$city[i]]]) } #> Radiation table successfully validated. #> Radiation table successfully validated. #> Radiation table successfully validated. # Create the plot with monthly global energies plt_hrad <- data_rad_list %>%    dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "city") %>%      ggplot(aes(y = Hrad, x = month, color = city)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   labs(subtitle = "Global energy") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom",         plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))  # Create the plot with monthly ratio between diffuse and direct rays plt_dgratio <- data_rad_list %>%    dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "city") %>%      ggplot(aes(y = DGratio, x = month, color = city)) +   geom_point() +   geom_line() +   labs(subtitle = "Ratio diffuse/global") +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none",         plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))  # Get legend containing cities legend_radiation <- cowplot::get_legend(plt_hrad) plt_hrad <- plt_hrad + theme(legend.position = "none")  # Combines the plots cowplot::plot_grid(   legend_radiation,   cowplot::plot_grid(plt_hrad, plt_dgratio, nrow = 1),   ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(1, 10) )

title: 2 - Understand ray discretization
text: can now easily run SamsaraLight ray-tracing model 9 virtual stands. sake tutorial, disable torus system, normally treats virtual plot infinitely replicated beyond borders. can done using advanced function run_sl_advanced() setting argument use_torus = FALSE. option intended advanced users used caution. Disabling torus system simply mean assuming plot surrounded open areas without trees (e.g. grasslands). Instead, torus system primarily compensates important modelling approximation: rays cast toward cells located inside virtual plot. consequence, rays normally reach cells outside plot simulated, leading underestimation intercepted radiation, especially trees located near plot borders. torus system addresses limitation assuming equivalent copies stand surround plot. assumption, trees outside plot intercept rays cast within plot way trees inside , thereby contributing total intercepted energy. approach provides practical approximation edge effects without explicitly simulating larger domain. detailed description torus system theoretical foundations, see original SamsaRaLight model paper Courbaud et al. (2003).
code: # Store SamsaraLight outputs in a list out_sl_list <- vector("list", length = nrow(exp_design))  for (i in 1:nrow(exp_design)) {      mod_design <- exp_design[i,]      # Run SamsaraLight as advanced user   # As the function is internal, we use SamsaRaLight::: for accessing    out_sl_list[[i]] <- SamsaRaLight:::run_sl_advanced(          # Simulation inputs     sl_stand = sl_stand_list[[i]],     monthly_radiations = data_rad_list[[mod_design$city]],          # Disable the torus system (only available in the run_sl_advanced() function)     use_torus = FALSE,          # Set detailed output to have information about ray-discretization and direct/diffuse outputs     detailed_output = TRUE,          # Activate parallel mode     parallel_mode = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE   )  }

what: Understand the discretisation of direct and diffuse rays
title: 2 - Understand ray discretization
text: discretisation rays given output$monthly_rays$rays run_sl() function output. Direct rays (direct = TRUE) follow Sun’s annual trajectory determined latitude, diffuse rays (direct = FALSE) discretized entire sky hemisphere. Ray directions defined azimut elevation (azimut height_angle, radians), incident energies (e_incident MJ/m2) derived monthly global radiation diffuse--global ratios distributed according ray geometry. Parameters relative discretisation diffuse direct rays can seen tweaked arguments advanced SamsaRaLight function : sl_run_advanced() (using Standard Overcast Sky default, contrast Uniform Overcast Sky, default minimum height angle rays 10°, direct rays start offset 0°, direct rays angle step 5° diffuse rays angle step 15°).  graph , two distinct azimuth–elevation patterns can observed. Direct rays form paraboloid-shaped distribution, representing Sun’s annual trajectory, whereas diffuse rays form regular grid points corresponding discretization entire sky hemisphere. maximum elevation angle direct rays increases decreasing latitude, reflecting higher solar path year consistently higher individual ray energies direct diffuse components. Sloped surfaces allow low-elevation, near-horizontal rays reach ground; however, north-facing slopes results strong truncation toward higher azimuth angles, primarily affecting many direct rays, south-facing slopes exhibit weaker truncation toward lower azimuth angles, mainly affecting smaller fraction diffuse rays.
code: out_sl_list %>%    purrr::map(~as.data.frame(as.list(.x$output$monthly_rays$rays))) %>%    dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "id_simu") %>%    dplyr::left_join(exp_design, by = "id_simu") %>%       ggplot(aes(x = azimut, y = height_angle,               color = e_incident, shape = direct)) +   geom_point() +   facet_grid(cols = vars(city), rows = vars(stand_geom)) +   scale_color_viridis_c() +   theme_bw()

code: # Get minimum PACL value  pacl_range <- out_sl_list %>%    purrr::map(~.x$output$light$cells$pacl) %>%    unlist() %>%    range()  # Store plots in a list plt_ground_list <- vector("list", nrow(exp_design)) legend <- NULL  for (i in 1:nrow(exp_design)) {      mod_design <- exp_design[i,]      # Plot the stand with light outputs   tmp_sl_plot <- plot(out_sl_list[[i]],                        what_cells = "relative",                       show_trees = FALSE)      # Change some ggplot2 features   tmp_sl_plot <- tmp_sl_plot +     labs(title = NULL,          subtitle = paste(mod_design$city, "|", mod_design$stand_geom)) +     theme(legend.position = "top",           axis.text = element_blank(),           plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5)) +     scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = pacl_range)             # Fetch the legend if it is not already done   # And remove the plot legend after that   if (is.null(legend)) {     tmp_sl_plot <- tmp_sl_plot + theme(legend.position = "right")     legend <- cowplot::get_legend(tmp_sl_plot)   }      tmp_sl_plot <- tmp_sl_plot + theme(legend.position = "none")           # Add the plot to the list   plt_ground_list[[i]] <- tmp_sl_plot }  # Gather all the plots using the cowplot package cowplot::plot_grid(   cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = plt_ground_list,                      nrow = 3, ncol = 3),   legend,   nrow = 1, rel_widths = c(5, 1) )

title: 4 - Understand ray transmission
text: previous vignettes, introduced build virtual stands simulate light interception forest stands. tutorial, focus key component simulations: way radiation attenuated rays cross tree crowns. SamsaRaLight, crown transmission can modelled using two alternative approaches: porous envelope model turbid medium model. models differ underlying assumptions way crown structure represented. choice transmission model parametrisation directly influences simulated light interception tree stand levels. Understanding effects therefore essential calibrating transmission models (see Tutorial 5) interpreting outputs. vignette, first present two transmission models. illustrate effects using simple virtual stands, first tree level stand level. Finally, discuss practical implications parameter selection calibration.

title: 4 - Understand ray transmission
text: SamsaRaLight, crown transmission controlled argument turbid_medium accessible advanced-user function sl_run_advanced(). Depending value, one two following models used. parameter set TRUE base sl_run() function, thus using turbid medium default model.

title: 4 - Understand ray transmission
text: turbid_medium = FALSE, crowns represented semi-transparent envelopes. time ray crosses crown, energy reduced fixed proportion defined crown openness. parameter defined crown_openness column tree inventory ranges within [0, 1] (required using sl_run() function). approach assumes : - Crown internal structure explicitly represented, - Attenuation independent path length inside crown, - Transmission spatially homogeneous within crown. consequence, porous envelope model provides simplified representation canopy structure. requires one intuitive parameter, account variations foliage density, neither path length ray within crowns.

title: 4 - Understand ray transmission
text: turbid_medium = TRUE, crowns treated homogeneous volumes filled scattering absorbing elements. case, light attenuation follows Beer–Lambert law depends distance travelled inside crown. Attenuation controlled leaf area density LAD mandatory column crown_lad tree inventory. parameters controlling turbid medium model can tweaked sl_run_advanced() function: extinction coefficient (default fixed 0.5) clumping factor (default fixed 1). approach explicitly links crown geometry foliage density light transmission. Rays travelling longer distances inside dense crowns experience stronger attenuation. turbid medium model therefore provides mechanistic representation radiative transfer. However, crown LAD value difficult calibrate, thus default value 0.5 commonly used. tutorial 5 shows use virtual sensors calibrate site-specific value LAD.

code: trees_inv <- trees_inv %>%       # Compute crown dimensions using allometries   # Taken from Courbaud et al. 2025 for spruce   dplyr::mutate(          h_m = exp(3.5300) * exp( - log( exp(3.5300) / 1.3 ) * exp( - 0.0767 * dbh_cm ) ),     hbase_m = ( 1 / (1 + exp(0.4880)) )  * h_m,          r_m = exp(-0.774) * ( dbh_cm ^ 0.525 ),     rs_m = r_m,     rn_m = r_m,     re_m = r_m,     rw_m = r_m,          volume_m3 = 0.5 * pi * r_m^2 * (h_m - hbase_m) # Volume of a paraboloid   ) plt_heights <- ggplot(trees_inv %>%                          dplyr::select(dbh_cm, h_m, hbase_m) %>%                         tidyr::pivot_longer(c(h_m, hbase_m),                                             names_to = "var",                                             values_to = "heights_m"),                        aes(y = heights_m, x = dbh_cm, linetype = var)) +   geom_line() +   theme_bw() +   theme(legend.position = "none") +   ylab("h_m (solid) and hbase_m (dotted)")  plt_diameter <- ggplot(trees_inv, aes(y = r_m * 2, x = dbh_cm)) +   geom_line() +   theme_bw()  plt_volume <- ggplot(trees_inv, aes(y = volume_m3, x = dbh_cm)) +   geom_line() +   theme_bw()  plt_heights | plt_diameter | plt_volume +   plot_layout()

code: # Get the monthly radiation data (data_bechefa location) latitude <- 50.04 longitude <- 5.2  data_rad <- get_monthly_radiations(latitude = latitude, longitude = longitude)  # Initialise the squared inventory zone core_polygon_df <- data.frame(   x = c(0, stand_size_x, stand_size_x, 0),   y = c(0, 0, stand_size_y, stand_size_y) ) out_trees <- vector("list", length = nrow(trees_inv_transmit))    for (i in trees_inv_transmit$id_simu) {      print(i)      # Create the virtual stand   tmp_stand <- SamsaRaLight::create_sl_stand(     trees_inv_transmit[i,],     cell_size = 5,     latitude = latitude,     slope = 0,     aspect = 0,     north2x = 90,     core_polygon_df = core_polygon_df,     verbose = FALSE   )      # Run SamsaraLight   tmp_out <- SamsaRaLight:::run_sl_advanced(     tmp_stand,     data_rad,     parallel_mode = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE,          # Transmission model and turbid medium parameters     turbid_medium = trees_inv_transmit$turbid_medium[i],     extinction_coef = 0.5,     clumping_factor = 1   )      # Get tree-level output   out_trees[[i]] <- tmp_out$output$light$trees }  out_trees <- out_trees %>%    dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "id_simu") %>%    dplyr::mutate(id_simu = as.integer(id_simu))

code: exp_design <- exp_design %>%    dplyr::mutate(     param_k = 1.5,     param_lambda = sqrt( (4 * batot_m2ha) / (pi * n_trees * gamma(1 + 2/param_k)) )   ) %>%    dplyr::mutate(     id_inv = row_number()   )  trees_inv <- vector("list", length = nrow(exp_design)) for (i in exp_design$id_inv) {      # Get diameter distribution from Weibull law   # Parameters are given by the above formula to reach the basal area given a J-shape and the number of trees   tmp_dbh <- rweibull(exp_design$n_trees[[i]],                        shape = exp_design$param_k[[i]],                        scale = exp_design$param_lambda[[i]]) * 100 # from meters in cm      # Create the trees with a random position   trees_inv[[i]] <- data.frame(          id_tree = 1:exp_design$n_trees[[i]],     dbh_cm = tmp_dbh,     x = runif(exp_design$n_trees[[i]], 0, stand_size_x),     y = runif(exp_design$n_trees[[i]], 0, stand_size_y),     species = "Picea abies"        ) %>%           # Estimate crown dimensions     dplyr::mutate(       crown_type = "P", # Paraboloidal crown              h_m = exp(3.5300) * exp( - log( exp(3.5300) / 1.3 ) * exp( - 0.0767 * dbh_cm ) ),       hbase_m = ( 1 / (1 + exp(0.4880)) )  * h_m,              r_m = exp(-0.774) * ( dbh_cm ^ 0.525 ),       rs_m = r_m,       rn_m = r_m,       re_m = r_m,       rw_m = r_m     ) }  trees_inv <- trees_inv %>%    dplyr::bind_rows(.id = "id_inv") %>%    dplyr::mutate(id_inv = as.integer(id_inv)) trees_inv %>%    dplyr::left_join(exp_design, by = "id_inv") %>%       dplyr::filter( (n_trees == 100 & batot_m2ha == 10) |                    (n_trees == 100 & batot_m2ha == 40) |                    (n_trees == 400 & batot_m2ha == 10) |                    (n_trees == 400 & batot_m2ha == 40)) %>%    ggplot(aes(x = dbh_cm,               linetype = as.factor(n_trees),               color = as.factor(batot_m2ha))) +   geom_density() +   theme_bw()

text: run series simulations trees assigned LAD value, spanning wide range. simulation, : - Run model using sensors_only = TRUE fasten drastically computation time, - Extract predicted PACL sensor locations, - Compute mean residual observed predicted values.
code: LADs <- seq(0.001, 5, by = 0.001) out_residuals <- vector("list", length(LADs)) i <- 0  time_start <- Sys.time()  for (lad in LADs) {      print(lad)   i <- i+1      # Set the tested LAD value   tmp_stand <- stand_cloture      tmp_stand$trees <- tmp_stand$trees %>%      dplyr::mutate(crown_lad = lad)       # Run SamsaraLight   out_sensors_calib <- SamsaRaLight::run_sl(          sl_stand = tmp_stand,      monthly_rad = SamsaRaLight::data_cloture20$radiations,     parallel_mode = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE,          sensors_only = TRUE # Here important because we run lot of simulations   )         # Compute the mean residuals   out_residuals[[i]] <-           dplyr::left_join(              SamsaRaLight::data_cloture20$sensors %>%          dplyr::select(id_sensor, pacl) %>%          dplyr::rename_at(vars(-"id_sensor"), ~paste0(., "_obs")),              out_sensors_calib$output$light$sensors %>%          dplyr::select(id_sensor, pacl) %>%          dplyr::rename_at(vars(-"id_sensor"), ~paste0(., "_pred")),       by = "id_sensor"            ) %>%           dplyr::mutate(       res = pacl_obs - pacl_pred     ) %>%           dplyr::summarise(res = mean(res)) %>%      dplyr::mutate(lad = lad, .before = res) }  time_end <- Sys.time() time_elapsed <- time_end - time_start  out_residuals <- dplyr::bind_rows(out_residuals) print(paste("Testing", length(LADs), "LAD values"))  #> [1] "Testing 5000 LAD values" print(time_elapsed) #> Time difference of 8.013666 mins

title: 5 - Virtual sensors
text: Virtual sensors provide practical first step calibrating foliage-related parameters SamsaRaLight. However, several limitations must considered. First, assume single mean LAD value trees. reality, LAD varies (1) species functional types, (2) Along ontogenetic gradients, (3) competition crown status. Second, crown geometry simplified model. Real trees exhibit strong crown plasticity asymmetry, influences light interception always captured idealized shapes. Third, LAD may vary sites function climate, soil conditions, disturbance history. Drought, pests, defoliation events can strongly affect foliage density. LAD controls light attenuation many process-based models, accurate estimation remains active research topic. Ongoing work aims developing individual-based LAD allometries dynamic formulations. models available, use calibrated mean site values, supported virtual sensors field measurements, provides robust transparent approach. absence field estimation light ground, fixed value 0.5m2/m3 can used trees’ LAD.

text: trees_inv data.frame one row per tree following columns: id_tree Unique identifier tree (numeric character, duplicates). x X position tree within stand (numeric, meters, planar coordinates). Optional lon lat provided. y Y position tree within stand (numeric, meters, planar coordinates). Optional lon lat provided. lon Longitude tree location (numeric, decimal degrees). Optional; provided lat, coordinates converted internally UTM planar coordinate system create_sl_stand(). lat Latitude tree location (numeric, decimal degrees). Optional; see lon. species Species name (character). dbh_cm Diameter breast height (1.3 m, cm). crown_type Type crown geometry. One "E", "P", "2E", "8E", "4P". h_m Total tree height (numeric, meters). hbase_m Height crown base (numeric, meters). hmax_m Height maximum crown radius (numeric, meters). code: create_sl_stand(   trees_inv,   cell_size,   latitude,   slope,   aspect,   north2x,   sensors = NULL,   core_polygon_df = NULL,   modify_polygon = c("none", "rect", "aarect"),   fill_around = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: trees_inv data.frame one row per tree. See check_inventory required structure validated columns. cell_size Numeric. Side length square cells composing stand (meters). latitude Numeric. Latitude stand (degrees). slope Numeric. Slope plot (degrees). aspect Numeric. Aspect slope, defined azimuth downslope direction, clockwise North (degrees). (0 degrees: North-facing slope, 90 degrees: East-facing slope, 180 degrees: South-facing slope, 270 degrees: West-facing slope) north2x Numeric. Clockwise angle North X-axis (degrees). default 90 degrees corresponds Y-axis oriented toward true North (0 degrees: x-axis points North, 90 degrees: x-axis points East, 180 degrees: x-axis points South, 270 degrees: x-axis points West). sensors Optional data.frame defining position height sensor within stand. See check_sensors required structure validated columns. core_polygon_df Optional data.frame defining core inventory polygon. Must contain columns x y. NULL, concave hull automatically computed tree positions. modify_polygon Character. Defines inventory polygon modified. One : "none": core polygon used provided computed. "rect": polygon replaced minimum enclosing rectangle. "aarect": polygon replaced axis-aligned enclosing rectangle. fill_around Logical. TRUE, trees added outside core polygon basal area per hectare full stand matches core inventory. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), messages warnings printed processing. FALSE, output silent.

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: named list following elements: trees Data.frame final tree inventory, including added trees fill_around = TRUE. structure matches inventory format validated check_inventory, additional derived variables required ray tracing. logical column added_to_fill indicates whether tree originates initial inventory added fill around inventory zone. core_polygon List describing inventory zone: df: data.frame polygon vertices sf: corresponding sf POLYGON modify_polygon: applied polygon modification transform List transformation filling information, including core area, target final basal area, number added trees, applied spatial transformations. geometry List describing stand geometry terrain parameters (cell size, number cells, slope, aspect, orientation).

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand
text: function supports sloping terrain coordinate system rotation, returns fully prepared stand ready use ray-tracing pipeline (see run_sl). returned trees data.frame conforms inventory format checked check_inventory, following controlled modifications: Tree vertical position z computed terrain slope aspect. Crown maximum radius height hmax_m computed fixed crown geometry conventions: "P" "4P": hmax_m = hbase_m "E" "4E": hmax_m = hbase_m + 0.5 * (h_m - hbase_m) crown types "2E" "8E", hmax_m must provided user. missing, column dbh_cm added filled NA. missing, crown interception properties (e.g. crown_openness, crown_lad) added using default values. input data.frames (trees_inv, sensors, core_polygon_df) automatically checked coordinate type: x y columns exist, assumed planar. lon lat columns exist, converted planar UTM coordinates automatically using create_xy_from_lonlat(). UTM projection (EPSG) determined mean coordinates trees_inv. inputs must share EPSG; otherwise, function stops error. conversion occurred, EPSG stored output. function ensures trees fall within core inventory polygon, applying small buffers necessary handle numerical precision issues. Invalid polygons automatically repaired possible. fill_around = TRUE, trees randomly sampled original inventory positioned outside core polygon target basal area per hectare reached full stand.

title: Create a virtual stand from a tree inventory — create_sl_stand

title: Run SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl
text: sl_stand object class "sl_stand" describing forest stand, created create_sl_stand. contains trees, sensors, terrain, grid geometry. monthly_radiations data.frame monthly horizontal radiation (Hrad, MJ m\(^{-2}\)) diffuse--global ratio (DGratio), typically obtained using get_monthly_radiations checked using check_monthly_radiations.. sensors_only Logical. TRUE, compute light interception sensors (much faster). detailed_output Logical. TRUE, output contains detailed diffuse/direct energies light datasets, full interception matrices interceptions output ray discretization monthy_rays. FALSE, total energies returned (recommended uses). parallel_mode logical. TRUE, ray–target computations parallelised using OpenMP. FALSE, model runs single-thread mode. SamsaRaLight uses OpenMP ray–target parallelisation. avoid competition OpenMP BLAS (matrix algebra libraries), BLAS automatically forced single-thread mode simulation. Using parallel_mode = TRUE strongly recommended large stands fine ray discretisation, computation time scales almost linearly number available CPU cores. n_threads integer NULL. Number CPU threads use parallel_mode = TRUE. NULL (default), OpenMP automatically selects number available cores. provided, must positive integer. verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages printed.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data_prenovel <- SamsaRaLight::data_prenovel  stand <- create_sl_stand(   trees = data_prenovel$trees,   sensors = data_prenovel$sensors,   cell_size = 5,   latitude = 45.8,   slope = 10,   aspect = 180,   north2x = 0 )  rad <- get_monthly_radiations(45.8, 3.1)  out <- run_sl(   sl_stand = stand,   monthly_radiations = rad )  out$light$trees } # }

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
code: run_sl_advanced(   sl_stand,   monthly_radiations,   sensors_only = FALSE,   use_torus = TRUE,   turbid_medium = TRUE,   extinction_coef = 0.5,   clumping_factor = 1,   trunk_interception = TRUE,   height_anglemin = 10,   direct_startoffset = 0,   direct_anglestep = 5,   diffuse_anglestep = 15,   soc = TRUE,   start_day = 1,   end_day = 365,   detailed_output = FALSE,   parallel_mode = FALSE,   n_threads = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
text: sl_stand object class "sl_stand" representing virtual stand. row tree required optional columns describing crown geometry, height, crown radius, crown openness, LAD, etc. See validate_sl_stand. monthly_radiations data.frame monthly horizontal radiation (Hrad) diffuse global ratio (DGratio), computed get_monthly_radiations. sensors_only logical, TRUE, compute interception sensors use_torus logical, TRUE, use torus system borders turbid_medium logical, TRUE, crowns considered turbid medium (using column crown_lad), else porous envelope (using column crown_openess) extinction_coef Numeric scalar. Leaf extinction coefficient controlling probability ray intercepted foliage. represents effective light attenuation per unit leaf area linked average leaf orientation. Higher values increase interception (default = 0.5). clumping_factor Numeric scalar controlling aggregation leaves within crown volume. value 1 corresponds homogeneous (random) foliage distribution; values < 1 indicate clumped foliage, values > 1 indicate regular spacing. modifies effective light interception turbid medium model (default = 1). trunk_interception logical, TRUE, account trunk interception height_anglemin numeric, minimum altitude angle rays (degrees) direct_startoffset numeric, starting angle first direct ray (degrees) direct_anglestep numeric, hour angle step direct rays (degrees) diffuse_anglestep numeric, hour angle step diffuse rays (degrees) soc logical, TRUE, use Standard Overcast Sky; FALSE, Uniform Overcast Sky start_day integer, first day vegetative period (1–365) end_day integer, last day vegetative period (1–365) detailed_output logical, TRUE, include detailed rays, energies, interception matrices parallel_mode logical. TRUE, ray–target computations parallelised using OpenMP. FALSE, model runs single-thread mode. n_threads integer NULL. Number CPU threads use parallel_mode = TRUE. NULL (default), OpenMP automatically selects number available cores. provided, must positive integer. verbose Logical; TRUE, informative messages printed.

title: Compute advanced SamsaRaLight radiative balance — run_sl_advanced
text: object class "sl_output" (list) containing: light: list simulation outputs trees, cells, sensors info: list run metadata (latitude, days, sky type, etc.) monthly_rays (detailed_output = TRUE): ray discretization per month interceptions (detailed_output = TRUE): tree/cell interception matrices
